mission.title: Install and Configure MongoDB Community Operator
mission.description: The MongoDB Community Operator allows you to deploy and manage MongoDB databases on Kubernetes clusters. It simplifies the management of MongoDB instances by automating tasks such as deployment, scaling, and backup. However, note that this operator is deprecated, and users are encouraged to migrate to the new MongoDB Kubernetes Operator repository.

steps.title: Add the MongoDB Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MongoDB Community Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official MongoDB repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the MongoDB Community Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the MongoDB Community Operator in the 'mongodb' namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace mongodb

helm install mongodb-community mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

```

> Creates a dedicated namespace for MongoDB.
> Installs the MongoDB Community Operator.

steps.title: Create a MongoDB Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a MongoDB instance using a Custom Resource (CR) definition.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
  persistent: true
EOF

```

> Creates a MongoDB ReplicaSet with 3 members.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.3
  persistent: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify the MongoDB Deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the MongoDB deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

kubectl get pods -n mongodb

```

> Lists the MongoDB instances in the 'mongodb' namespace.
> Lists the pods to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MongoDB Community Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MongoDB Community Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete your MongoDB instances and any data stored in them.

```bash

helm uninstall mongodb-community --namespace mongodb

kubectl delete namespace mongodb

```

> Uninstalls the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MongoDB Community Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your MongoDB data. This step will upgrade the operator and verify the deployment afterward.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o json > mongodb-backup.json

helm upgrade mongodb-community mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

```

> Backup current MongoDB configuration.
> Upgrades the MongoDB Community Operator.
> Verify the MongoDB instance status after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o json > mongodb-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'MongoDB CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition (CRD) for MongoDB has not been installed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator/v0.13.0/deploy/crds/mongodb.com_mongodb_crd.yaml' to install the CRD.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n mongodb' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the MongoDB pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resources in your cluster or adjust the resource requests in the MongoDB CR.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to MongoDB'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB service is not reachable, possibly due to network policies or incorrect service configuration.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration with 'kubectl get svc -n mongodb' and ensure network policies allow traffic.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for MongoDB 5.0.3, improved logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from a previous version, ensure to follow the migration guide provided in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the MongoDB instance listed with 'kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb' and the pods should be in 'Running' status.

  version: 5.0.3
  persistent: true
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
